Heart & Lung Anatomy Escape Room

DPTD 732 · Cardiopulmonary Anatomy Review

Time: 00:00

Ready to begin?

You and your team have been "sealed in" until you can trace oxygen's full journey through the heart and lungs. At each station, complete every activity correctly to reveal that station's locked word, then type it in to unlock the next door. Good luck!


Station 1 of 8

Getting Oriented

Before air or blood can go anywhere, you need to know where the heart sits and how air first enters the body. Review the handout section on heart location/pericardium and the upper respiratory tract if you need a refresher.
Finish the word search and the multiple choice below — each reveals half of your station code.

🔎 Word Search

Tap the first letter of a word, then tap its last letter (words run left-right, top-bottom, or diagonally). Tap the same letter twice to cancel your selection.

✅ Multiple Choice

Click the correct answer for each question.
Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word above.

Word Search reveals the first half; Multiple Choice reveals the second half. Together they spell a 4-letter heart landmark found at the 5th intercostal space, midclavicular line.
Station 2 of 8

Layers Within Layers

The heart wall and the lungs are both wrapped in protective, low-friction layers. Notice how similarly they're organized.

🔗 Matching

Click a term on the left, then click its matching description on the right. Complete all 5 pairs to unlock the Codebreaker below.

🔐 Codebreaker

🔒 Complete the matching activity above to unlock these questions.
A word is locked behind the tiles below. Answer each question correctly to reveal another piece of it.
Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word.

Complete the matching activity first, then answer all 5 questions to reveal every letter. The word is 10 letters long and names the heart layer made of cardiac muscle.
Station 3 of 8

Map the Heart

Label the diagram using what you know about the heart's chambers, valves, and major vessels.

🖍️ Diagram Labeling

Tap a label below, then tap the empty slot next to its matching number (or drag it there). Each numbered dot on the heart connects — via a dotted line — to a slot in the column beside it. Label every structure, then click "Check Labels" to unlock the quick check below.

🩺 Quick Check: Valve Timing

🔒 Correctly label all 13 structures above to unlock these questions.
Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word above.

Correctly label all 13 diagram structures to unlock two quick questions — together they reveal an 8-letter word for what the heart has four of.
Station 4 of 8

Follow the Flow

Blood and air both travel one-way circuits. Put each pathway in the correct order.
Perfectly order both sequences below — each reveals part of your station code.

🩸 Sequence: Blood Flow

Use the arrows to arrange these structures in the order deoxygenated blood travels, starting from the vena cavae, until it becomes oxygenated blood reaching the aorta.

    💨 Sequence: Airflow

    Arrange these structures in the order air travels from the outside world to the site of gas exchange.
      Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word above.

      Perfectly ordering both sequences reveals a 7-letter word: tiny air sacs surrounded by capillaries, where gas exchange happens.
      Station 5 of 8

      The Heart's Own Blood Supply

      Even the heart needs to be fed. The coronary arteries branch directly off the aorta to supply the myocardium.

      🔗 Matching

      Click a vessel on the left, then click its matching description on the right. Complete all 4 pairs to unlock the Codebreaker below.

      🔢 Codebreaker

      🔒 Complete the matching activity above to unlock these questions.
      A 3-letter artery abbreviation is locked behind the tiles below. Answer each question correctly to reveal another letter.
      Station Code: Enter the fully revealed 3-letter abbreviation.

      Complete the matching activity first, then answer all 3 questions to reveal every letter. It's a branch of the left coronary artery that supplies most of the left ventricle.
      Station 6 of 8

      Crossword: Inside the Thorax

      Solve the full crossword below — every across and every down entry is a real anatomy term. Solving the entire grid reveals your station code.

      Across

      1. 1. Windpipe; contains cartilaginous rings, bifurcates into the main bronchi at the angle of Louis (T4–T5)
      2. 2. Membrane that lines the pleural cavities and covers the lungs
      3. 4. ___ artery/vein: vessels carrying blood to and from the lungs
      4. 6. Coronary ___: wide vein that drains the heart's own blood supply into the right atrium
      5. 7. Upper heart chamber that receives blood before passing it to a ventricle
      6. 9. General term for a structure (like the mitral or aortic) that ensures one-way blood flow through the heart

      Down

      1. 1. The chest cavity; contains the heart, lungs, and great vessels
      2. 3. Largest artery in the body; receives blood from the left ventricle
      3. 5. Voice box; connects the pharynx to the trachea
      4. 6. Wall of cardiac muscle separating the right and left ventricles
      5. 8. ___ valve: the left atrioventricular (bicuspid) valve
      6. 9. Lower heart chamber; the left one has thicker, more muscular walls than the right
      Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word above.

      Solve the entire crossword correctly (all 12 words) to reveal a 6-letter word for the chest cavity — it's 1-Down.
      Station 7 of 8

      Sort & Match

      Time to zoom out. This station checks how well you can connect individual structures to the bigger picture — which circuit they belong to, and what they actually do.
      Finish the sorting activity and the memory match below — each reveals part of your station code.

      🗂️ Sort the Circuit

      Tap a structure below, then tap the bin it belongs in (or drag it there). Sort all 10 correctly.
      Pulmonary Circulation
      Systemic Circulation

      🧠 Memory Match: Structure & Function

      Flip two cards at a time. Match every structure to its function.
      Station Code: Enter the fully revealed word above.

      Sort all 10 structures into their correct circuit, then match all 6 structure/function pairs, to reveal an 11-letter word for the two closed-loop pathways blood follows through the body.
      Station 8 of 8 — Final Station

      Clinical Reasoning & Final Escape

      You haven't covered pathology or intervention yet — these questions only require reasoning from structure and function.

      🩺 Clinical Scenarios

      Answer all 3 correctly to unlock the final lock below.

      🔤 Final Lock: Guess the Word

      🔒 Answer all 3 clinical questions above correctly to unlock the final lock.
      One 6-letter word ties everything you did today together — it's what every structure in this room exists to deliver. Guess it to escape. After each guess: green = right letter, right spot; yellow = right letter, wrong spot; grey = not in the word.
      It's the molecule alveoli hand off to capillaries, that travels through the left heart, out the aorta, and to every tissue in the body.

      🎉 You Escaped!

      Nice work tracing oxygen's full path — from the upper airway, through the alveoli, into the left heart, and out to the body (and the reverse trip for carbon dioxide).
